pages.title: About me - Michelle Scrogham MP
headings: h2: Standing up for our communities
pages.text_excerpt: About me - Michelle Scrogham MP Skip to main content Home About My priorities News Get in touch More Menu About me Michelle Scrogham MP for Barrow and Furness It was the honour of my life to be elected as Member of Parliament for Barrow and Furness and to then be selected to be on to the Defence Committee. I will always be a strong voice in Parliament for my constituency and for the interests of our brave armed forces and veterans. My experience I’m a relative newcomer to politics, not a career politician. I set up and ran a high street retail and online business. I’m deeply involved in the community, volunteering with many of the local festivals and organising the largest, Ulverston Dickensian Festival, for the past 8 years. I’m proud to be part of this volunteer committee which brings approx. £3 million to Furness each year. My early career was in banking, working locally in Barrow, Dalton, Millom and Ulverston and ensured I was well known to the community. During my years at the bank I was shocked at the levels of discrimination and never one to back down in the face of injustice, I became a union rep and eventually the Jointly Accredited Rep for the North West Region, and Chair of the Banking Insurance & Finance Union North West. I spent many years representing members facing redundancy, bullying, harassment and disciplinary issues, and regularly spoke on their behalf at BIFU’s National Annual Conference. Standing up for our communities My drive in life is Community. I believe it’s the bedrock of a lot of the best things in life and when we work together we can achieve incredible things. I’m passionate about our High Streets, I see them as the heart of the Community along with being the largest employer in the UK. This, and my love of working with the public was behind starting my business in 2005. I’ve worked hard over the years to deliver benefits to the wider community wherever possible, enriching lives and improving services in whatever role or job I’ve held, be that in business, as a school Governor, banking, union rep, festival organiser or local government. I will continue to do that and much more as your Member of Parliament. Stay updated Sign up with your email address to receive all news and updates from Michelle. pages.title: My priorities - Michelle Scrogham MP
pages.text_excerpt: My priorities - Michelle Scrogham MP Skip to main content Home About My priorities News Get in touch More Menu My priorities I’m about community and the success of Furness. We’ve been let down and left behind, with our communities becoming some of the poorest in the country. We need strength and a voice that will fight for Furness. ​My priorities: Michelle at FGH with petition signatures Protect our NHS and put it back on its feet again after years of neglect. That includes making sure that Furness General Hospital is made a priority for investment and that services are improved. Barrow Rising adding greenery to town centre Regenerate our High Streets. I am passionate about the role that our town High Streets can play in building community. So many people rely on our High Streets and I want to see them thriving again across Barrow and Furness. Michelle with Defence Committee Support our defence industry, brave armed-forces personnel and veterans. Through my position on the Defence Committee, as Chair of the AUKUS All Party Parliamentary Group, and as officer in the All-Party Defence Group I will stand up for our country’s important defence industry, our armed forces and veterans in Parliament. Michelle at Spirit Energy / MNZ Peak Cluster Unlock potential for green jobs in Furness. Our unique geography and manufacturing expertise means we can become a national leader in the green-energy transition which will bring jobs and investment. I will push forward plans for new hydrogen power innovation, a floating solar park, upgrades to the offshore wind industry, and a carbon storage facility making use of the depleted gas fields in Morecambe Bay. Michelle on a local bus Get buses, roads, and rail working for you . Our towns have some of the worst transport links in the country. The A590 and A595 are not fit for purpose and our train and bus infrastructure is a joke – all this is holding us back. For decades, major road upgrades and rail projects have been promised, but Tory governments in Westminster have failed to deliver. I will unlock better infrastructure for our towns.
